1 --- 2 title: "Building Channel Plugins" 3 sidebarTitle: "Channel Plugins" 4 summary: "Step-by-step guide to building a messaging channel plugin for OpenClaw" 5 read_when: 6 - You are building a new messaging channel plugin 7 - You want to connect OpenClaw to a messaging platform 8 - You need to understand the ChannelPlugin adapter surface 9 --- 10 11 # Building Channel Plugins 12 13 This guide walks through building a channel plugin that connects OpenClaw to a 14 messaging platform. By the end you will have a working channel with DM security, 15 pairing, reply threading, and outbound messaging. 16 17 18 If you have not built any OpenClaw plugin before, read 19 [Getting Started](/plugins/building-plugins) first for the basic package 20 structure and manifest setup. 21 22 23 ## How channel plugins work 24 25 Channel plugins do not need their own send/edit/react tools. OpenClaw keeps one 26 shared `message` tool in core. Your plugin owns: 27 28 - **Config** — account resolution and setup wizard 29 - **Security** — DM policy and allowlists 30 - **Pairing** — DM approval flow 31 - **Session grammar** — how provider-specific conversation ids map to base chats, thread ids, and parent fallbacks 32 - **Outbound** — sending text, media, and polls to the platform 33 - **Threading** — how replies are threaded 34 35 Core owns the shared message tool, prompt wiring, the outer session-key shape, 36 generic `:thread:` bookkeeping, and dispatch. 37 38 If your platform stores extra scope inside conversation ids, keep that parsing 39 in the plugin with `messaging.resolveSessionConversation(...)`. That is the 40 canonical hook for mapping `rawId` to the base conversation id, optional thread 41 id, explicit `baseConversationId`, and any `parentConversationCandidates`. 42 When you return `parentConversationCandidates`, keep them ordered from the 43 narrowest parent to the broadest/base conversation. 44 45 Bundled plugins that need the same parsing before the channel registry boots 46 can also expose a top-level `session-key-api.ts` file with a matching 47 `resolveSessionConversation(...)` export. Core uses that bootstrap-safe surface 48 only when the runtime plugin registry is not available yet. 49 50 `messaging.resolveParentConversationCandidates(...)` remains available as a 51 legacy compatibility fallback when a plugin only needs parent fallbacks on top 52 of the generic/raw id. If both hooks exist, core uses 53 `resolveSessionConversation(...).parentConversationCandidates` first and only 54 falls back to `resolveParentConversationCandidates(...)` when the canonical hook 55 omits them. 56 57 ## Approvals and channel capabilities 58 59 Most channel plugins do not need approval-specific code. 60 61 - Core owns same-chat `/approve`, shared approval button payloads, and generic fallback delivery. 62 - Prefer one `approvalCapability` object on the channel plugin when the channel needs approval-specific behavior. 63 - `approvalCapability.authorizeActorAction` and `approvalCapability.getActionAvailabilityState` are the canonical approval-auth seam. 64 - Use `outbound.shouldSuppressLocalPayloadPrompt` or `outbound.beforeDeliverPayload` for channel-specific payload lifecycle behavior such as hiding duplicate local approval prompts or sending typing indicators before delivery. 65 - Use `approvalCapability.delivery` only for native approval routing or fallback suppression. 66 - Use `approvalCapability.render` only when a channel truly needs custom approval payloads instead of the shared renderer. 67 - If a channel can infer stable owner-like DM identities from existing config, use `createResolvedApproverActionAuthAdapter` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-runtime` to restrict same-chat `/approve` without adding approval-specific core logic. 68 - If a channel needs native approval delivery, keep channel code focused on target normalization and transport hooks. Use `createChannelExecApprovalProfile`, `createChannelNativeOriginTargetResolver`, `createChannelApproverDmTargetResolver`, `createApproverRestrictedNativeApprovalCapability`, and `createChannelNativeApprovalRuntime` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-runtime` so core owns request filtering, routing, dedupe, expiry, and gateway subscription. 69 - Native approval channels must route both `accountId` and `approvalKind` through those helpers. `accountId` keeps multi-account approval policy scoped to the right bot account, and `approvalKind` keeps exec vs plugin approval behavior available to the channel without hardcoded branches in core. 70 - `createApproverRestrictedNativeApprovalAdapter` still exists as a compatibility wrapper, but new code should prefer the capability builder and expose `approvalCapability` on the plugin. 71 72 Auth-only channels can usually stop at the default path: core handles approvals and the plugin just exposes outbound/auth capabilities. Native approval channels such as Matrix, Slack, Telegram, and custom chat transports should use the shared native helpers instead of rolling their own approval lifecycle. 73 74 ## Walkthrough 75 76 77 78 79 Create the standard plugin files. The `channel` field in `package.json` is 80 what makes this a channel plugin: 81 82 83 ```json package.json 84 { 85 "name": "@myorg/openclaw-acme-chat", 86 "version": "1.0.0", 87 "type": "module", 88 "openclaw": { 89 "extensions": ["./index.ts"], 90 "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts", 91 "channel": { 92 "id": "acme-chat", 93 "label": "Acme Chat", 94 "blurb": "Connect OpenClaw to Acme Chat." 95 } 96 } 97 } 98 ``` 99 100 ```json openclaw.plugin.json 101 { 102 "id": "acme-chat", 103 "kind": "channel", 104 "channels": ["acme-chat"], 105 "name": "Acme Chat", 106 "description": "Acme Chat channel plugin", 107 "configSchema": { 108 "type": "object", 109 "additionalProperties": false, 110 "properties": { 111 "acme-chat": { 112 "type": "object", 113 "properties": { 114 "token": { "type": "string" }, 115 "allowFrom": { 116 "type": "array", 117 "items": { "type": "string" } 118 } 119 } 120 } 121 } 122 } 123 } 124 ``` 125 126 127 128 129 130 The `ChannelPlugin` interface has many optional adapter surfaces. Start with 131 the minimum — `id` and `setup` — and add adapters as you need them. 132 133 Create `src/channel.ts`: 134 135 ```typescript src/channel.ts 136 import { 137 createChatChannelPlugin, 138 createChannelPluginBase, 139 } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core"; 140 import type { OpenClawConfig } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core"; 141 import { acmeChatApi } from "./client.js"; // your platform API client 142 143 type ResolvedAccount = { 144 accountId: string | null; 145 token: string; 146 allowFrom: string[]; 147 dmPolicy: string | undefined; 148 }; 149 150 function resolveAccount(

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile all this information into a detailed report:

## OpenClaw Bundled Channel Plugins - Complete Structure Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the OpenClaw repository, here's everything you need to know about the bundled channel plugin structure:

### 1. PLUGIN TREE LOCATION

All bundled channel plugins live in the **`/extensions/`** directory. The channel plugins identified include:
- **IRC** (`extensions/irc/`)
- **Nostr** (`extensions/nostr/`)
- **Matrix** (`extensions/matrix/`)
- **Discord** (`extensions/discord/`)
- **Zalo** (`extensions/zalo/`)
- Plus others like Feishu, BlueBubbles, etc.

**Extensions Boundary Documentation:**
- `/extensions/AGENTS.md` (also symlinked as `CLAUDE.md`)

---

### 2. COMPLETE FILE STRUCTURE - IRC PLUGIN

**Location:** `/extensions/irc/`

**Root-level files (entry points & configuration):**
```
extensions/irc/
├── index.ts                      # Channel plugin entry point (exports ircPlugin)
├── setup-entry.ts                # Setup/onboarding entry point
├── api.ts                         # Public API barrel (exports accounts, setup-surface)
├── channel-config-api.ts          # Channel-specific config API
├── openclaw.plugin.json           # Plugin manifest
└── package.json                   # NPM package metadata
```

**Source directory** (`extensions/irc/src/`):
```
src/
├── accounts.ts                    # Account resolution & listing
├── accounts.test.ts               # Account tests
├── channel.ts                     # Core ChannelPlugin implementation
├── channel.test.ts                # Channel tests
├── client.ts                       # IRC client wrapper
├── client.test.ts                 # Client tests
├── config-schema.ts               # Zod config validation schemas
├── config-schema.test.ts           # Schema validation tests
├── config-ui-hints.ts             # UI hints for configuration
├── connect-options.ts             # Connection option handling
├── connect-options.test.ts         # Connection tests
├── control-chars.ts               # IRC control character handling
├── control-chars.test.ts          # Control char tests
├── inbound.ts                      # Inbound message processing
├── inbound.behavior.test.ts        # Inbound behavior tests
├── inbound.policy.test.ts          # Inbound policy tests
├── monitor.ts                      # Channel monitoring/event loop
├── monitor.test.ts                 # Monitor tests
├── normalize.ts                    # Message/target normalization
├── normalize.test.ts               # Normalization tests
├── policy.ts                       # Security & access policies
├── policy.test.ts                  # Policy tests
├── probe.ts                        # Connection probing
├── probe.test.ts                   # Probe tests
├── protocol.ts                     # IRC protocol implementation
├── protocol.test.ts                # Protocol tests
├── runtime-api.ts                  # Public runtime API barrel (re-exports from SDK)
├── runtime.ts                      # Runtime singleton management
├── send.ts                         # Outbound message sending
├── send.test.ts                    # Send tests
├── setup-core.ts                   # Setup logic (non-UI)
├── setup-surface.ts                # Setup UI/wizard
├── setup.test.ts                   # Setup tests
└── types.ts                        # TypeScript type definitions
```

---

### 3. COMPLETE FILE STRUCTURE - NOSTR PLUGIN

**Location:** `/extensions/nostr/`

**Root-level files:**
```
extensions/nostr/
├── index.ts                       # Channel plugin entry point
├── setup-entry.ts                 # Setup entry point
├── api.ts                          # Public API barrel (re-exports runtime-api)
├── runtime-api.ts                  # Public runtime API barrel (re-exports SDK)
├── setup-api.ts                    # Setup API exports (setup surface components)
├── openclaw.plugin.json            # Plugin manifest
├── package.json                    # NPM package with dependencies
├── CHANGELOG.md                    # Version history
└── README.md                       # Plugin documentation
```

**Source directory** (`extensions/nostr/src/`):
```
src/
├── channel.ts                      # Core ChannelPlugin implementation
├── channel.test.ts                 # Channel tests
├── channel.inbound.test.ts         # Inbound-specific tests
├── channel.outbound.test.ts        # Outbound-specific tests
├── config-schema.ts                # Config validation schemas
├── default-relays.ts               # Default Nostr relay list
├── metrics.ts                       # Metrics collection
├── nostr-bus.ts                    # Main Nostr event bus
├── nostr-bus.test.ts               # Bus tests
├── nostr-bus.fuzz.test.ts          # Fuzz testing
├── nostr-bus.inbound.test.ts       # Inbound bus tests
├── nostr-bus.integration.test.ts   # Integration tests
├── nostr-profile.ts                # Profile management
├── nostr-profile.test.ts           # Profile tests
├── nostr-profile.fuzz.test.ts      # Profile fuzz tests
├── nostr-profile-http.ts           # HTTP handler for profile updates
├── nostr-profile-http.test.ts      # HTTP handler tests
├── nostr-profile-import.ts         # Profile import logic
├── nostr-profile-import.test.ts    # Import tests
├── nostr-state-store.ts            # State persistence
├── nostr-state-store.test.ts       # State store tests
├── runtime.ts                      # Runtime singleton
├── seen-tracker.ts                 # Event deduplication
├── session-route.ts                # Outbound session routing
├── setup-surface.ts                # Setup UI/wizard
├── test-fixtures.ts                # Test helpers
└── types.ts                        # Type definitions
```

**Test directory** (`extensions/nostr/test/`):
```
test/
└── setup.ts                        # Test setup utilities
```

---

### 4. MANIFEST FILES (openclaw.plugin.json)

**IRC Manifest:**
```json
{
  "id": "irc",
  "channels": ["irc"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**Nostr Manifest:**
```json
{
  "id": "nostr",
  "channels": ["nostr"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**Key fields in manifests:**
- `id`: Plugin identifier (matches directory name)
- `channels`: Array of channel type(s) this plugin provides
- `configSchema`: JSON Schema for top-level plugin configuration

---

### 5. PACKAGE.JSON METADATA

**IRC package.json:**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/irc",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw IRC channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "detailLabel": "IRC",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "docsLabel": "irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing and pairing controls.",
      "aliases": ["internet-relay-chat"],
      "systemImage": "network"
    }
  }
}
```

**Nostr package.json:**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/nostr",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw Nostr channel plugin for NIP-04 encrypted DMs",
  "type": "module",
  "dependencies": {
    "nostr-tools": "^2.23.3"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "openclaw": "workspace:*"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "openclaw": ">=2026.4.1-beta.1"
  },
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "nostr",
      "label": "Nostr",
      "selectionLabel": "Nostr (NIP-04 DMs)",
      "docsPath": "/channels/nostr",
      "docsLabel": "nostr",
      "blurb": "Decentralized protocol; encrypted DMs via NIP-04.",
      "order": 55,
      "quickstartAllowFrom": true
    },
    "install": {
      "npmSpec": "@openclaw/nostr",
      "defaultChoice": "npm",
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    },
    "release": {
      "publishToNpm": true
    }
  }
}
```

**Key package.json structure:**
- `name`: NPM package name (convention: `@openclaw/<plugin-id>`)
- `type`: "module" (ES modules)
- `openclaw.extensions`: Entry point file(s)
- `openclaw.setupEntry`: Setup wizard entry point
- `openclaw.channel`: Channel metadata (label, docs, ordering, etc.)

---

### 6. ENTRY POINT FILES

**index.ts pattern (IRC):**
```typescript
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "irc",
  name: "IRC",
  description: "IRC channel plugin",
  plugin: ircPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setIrcRuntime,
});
```

**index.ts pattern (Nostr):**
```typescript
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { nostrPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
// ... more imports
export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "nostr",
  name: "Nostr",
  description: "Nostr DM channel plugin via NIP-04",
  plugin: nostrPlugin,
  setRuntime: setNostrRuntime,
  registerFull(api) {
    // Optional: register HTTP routes, etc.
    const httpHandler = createNostrProfileHttpHandler({...});
    api.registerHttpRoute({...});
  },
});
```

**setup-entry.ts pattern:**
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(ircPlugin);
```

---

### 7. KEY API BARREL FILES

**api.ts** - Public interface for bundled plugins
- IRC: Exports accounts and setup-surface components
- Nostr: Re-exports from runtime-api only

**runtime-api.ts** - Public runtime facade
- IRC: Re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` (irc-specific SDK)
- Nostr: Re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr` (nostr-specific SDK)

**setup-api.ts** (Nostr only)
- Exports: `nostrSetupAdapter`, `nostrSetupWizard` from setup-surface

---

### 8. CORE IMPLEMENTATION FILES

**channel.ts**
- Implements the main `ChannelPlugin` interface
- Creates the plugin using `createChatChannelPlugin()`
- Integrates account resolution, monitoring, inbound/outbound handling
- Configures directory adapters, status reporting, pairing controls

**runtime.ts**
- Manages plugin runtime singleton with `getPluginRuntime()`/`setPluginRuntime()`
- Provides configuration, logging, account access to the channel implementation

**setup-core.ts & setup-surface.ts**
- `setup-core.ts`: Non-UI setup logic (accounts validation, probing)
- `setup-surface.ts`: Interactive setup wizard UI components

**config-schema.ts**
- Zod validation schemas for all channel-level and account-level configs
- Defines `GroupPolicy`, `DmPolicy`, `AccountConfig`, etc.
- Example: IRC has `IrcAccountConfig`, `IrcGroupSchema`, `IrcNickServConfig`

**types.ts**
- TypeScript interfaces for config, accounts, and channel-specific types
- Example IRC types: `IrcAccountConfig`, `ResolvedIrcAccount`, `IrcProbe`
- Example Nostr types: `NostrAccountConfig`, `ResolvedNostrAccount`, `NostrProfile`

---

### 9. EXTENSIONS BOUNDARY RULES (from AGENTS.md)

Key constraints for plugin development:

1. **Import from public SDK only:**
   - `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` (typed subpaths)
   - Local barrel exports like `./api.ts`, `./runtime-api.ts`
   - Do NOT import from `src/**` or `src/channels/**` in core

2. **Keep plugin metadata accurate:**
   - `openclaw.plugin.json` and `package.json` must be valid
   - Used for discovery without executing plugin code

3. **Public contract files:**
   - Docs: `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`, `docs/plugins/manifest.md`
   - Definition files: `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`, `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`

4. **Architecture:**
   - Setup plugins define configuration UI and validation
   - Channel plugins handle inbound/outbound messaging and accounts
   - Runtime APIs provide typed access to plugin state during execution

---

### 10. COMMON PATTERNS ACROSS CHANNEL PLUGINS

**All channel plugins follow this structure:**
```
extensions/<channel-id>/
├── index.ts                    (entry, exports plugin)
├── setup-entry.ts              (setup wizard entry)
├── api.ts                       (public barrel)
├── runtime-api.ts              (runtime barrel)
├── [setup-api.ts]              (optional setup barrel)
├── [channel-config-api.ts]     (optional config-specific API)
├── openclaw.plugin.json        (manifest)
├── package.json                (with openclaw metadata)
├── [README.md]                 (optional docs)
├── [CHANGELOG.md]              (optional version history)
└── src/
    ├── channel.ts              (ChannelPlugin implementation)
    ├── runtime.ts              (runtime singleton)
    ├── channel.test.ts         (channel tests)
    ├── config-schema.ts        (Zod schemas)
    ├── types.ts                (TypeScript interfaces)
    ├── setup-core.ts           (setup logic)
    ├── setup-surface.ts        (setup UI)
    ├── accounts.ts             (account resolution)
    ├── monitor.ts              (event loop)
    ├── send.ts                 (outbound)
    ├── probe.ts                (connection testing)
    ├── policy.ts               (security policies)
    ├── normalize.ts            (message normalization)
    └── [many more .ts/.test.ts files depending on complexity]
```

---

### 11. SUMMARIZED DEPENDENCIES

**IRC dependencies:** None listed (uses built-in Node IRC)

**Nostr dependencies:** 
- `nostr-tools` (^2.23.3) - NIP compliance and crypto

**Shared peer dependency:**
- `openclaw` (>=2026.4.1) - OpenClaw framework

---

This structure ensures plugins are:
- **Self-contained** in their directory
- **Discoverable** via manifest
- **Isolated** from core internals
- **Testable** with comprehensive test files
- **Configurable** via Zod schemas
- **Reusable** through barrel exports

## OpenClaw Bundled Channel Plugins - Complete Structure Report

### 1. PLUGIN TREE LOCATION

**Key package.json structure:**

- `type`: "module" (ES modules)
- `openclaw.extensions`: Entry point file(s)

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me create the detailed summary:

## Deep Dive: IRC Channel Plugin Implementation

I've thoroughly analyzed the IRC plugin and compared it with other channel plugins (Nostr, Discord). Here's the complete implementation pattern:

### 1. **File Structure Overview**

The IRC plugin has a clean, modular architecture:

```
/extensions/irc/
├── index.ts                      # Main entry point
├── api.ts                        # Public API barrel (accounts, setup-surface)
├── channel-config-api.ts         # Config schema export
├── runtime-api.ts                # Private runtime barrel (SDK re-exports)
├── setup-entry.ts                # Setup plugin entry
├── package.json                  # Package metadata + openclaw config
├── openclaw.plugin.json          # Plugin manifest
└── src/
    ├── channel.ts                # Core ChannelPlugin definition
    ├── runtime.ts                # Runtime store (getIrcRuntime, setIrcRuntime)
    ├── types.ts                  # TypeScript type definitions
    ├── accounts.ts               # Account resolution & listing
    ├── send.ts                   # Outbound message sending
    ├── inbound.ts                # Inbound message handling
    ├── monitor.ts                # Connection monitoring & lifecycle
    ├── client.ts                 # Raw IRC protocol client
    ├── protocol.ts               # IRC message parsing/serialization
    ├── normalize.ts              # Target/entry normalization
    ├── policy.ts                 # Security & access control
    ├── config-schema.ts          # Zod schema definitions
    ├── setup-core.ts             # Core setup logic
    ├── setup-surface.ts          # Interactive setup wizard
    ├── probe.ts                  # Connection testing
    ├── control-chars.ts          # IRC control character handling
    ├── connect-options.ts        # Connection configuration builder
    ├── config-ui-hints.ts        # UI hints for config
    └── *.test.ts                 # 15+ test files
```

### 2. **Registration Pattern**

**`index.ts` - Main Entry Point:**
The plugin registers via `defineChannelPluginEntry()`:
- Exports the `ircPlugin` from `channel.ts`
- Exports the `setIrcRuntime` from `runtime.ts`
- Metadata: `id: "irc"`, `name: "IRC"`, description
- Returns default export with `id`, `name`, `description`, `plugin`, `setRuntime`

**`package.json` - OpenClaw Metadata:**
```json
{
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "detailLabel": "IRC",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing...",
      "systemImage": "network"
    }
  }
}
```

**`setup-entry.ts`:**
Simply wraps the plugin for setup via `defineSetupPluginEntry(ircPlugin)`

### 3. **Core Channel Plugin Definition** (`src/channel.ts`)

The `ircPlugin` is created via `createChatChannelPlugin()` with these major sections:

#### **a) Base Configuration**
```typescript
base: {
  id: "irc",
  meta: { ...getChatChannelMeta("irc"), quickstartAllowFrom: true },
  setup: ircSetupAdapter,           // How to apply user input to config
  setupWizard: ircSetupWizard,      // Interactive setup prompts
  capabilities: {
    chatTypes: ["direct", "group"],
    media: true,
    blockStreaming: true
  },
  reload: { configPrefixes: ["channels.irc"] },
  configSchema: IrcChannelConfigSchema // Zod schema
}
```

#### **b) Config Adapter** (resolves account, validates setup)
- `listAccountIds()` - List all IRC accounts
- `resolveAccount()` - Get account config with defaults
- `isConfigured()` - Check if account has required fields (host, nick)
- `describeAccount()` - Human-readable account summary
- `defaultAccountId` - Fallback account

#### **c) Groups** (channel access control)
```typescript
groups: {
  resolveRequireMention({ cfg, accountId, groupId })
  resolveToolPolicy({ cfg, accountId, groupId })
}
```
Controls per-channel mention requirements and tool policies

#### **d) Messaging** (target normalization)
```typescript
messaging: {
  normalizeTarget: normalizeIrcMessagingTarget,  // "#channel" or "nick"
  targetResolver: {
    looksLikeId: looksLikeIrcTargetId,
    hint: "<#channel|nick>"
  }
}
```

#### **e) Resolver** (convert user input to message targets)
- `resolveTargets()` - Map inputs to IRC targets (channels get `#`, nicks as-is)
- Validates kind (group vs user)

#### **f) Directory** (peer/group listing)
- `listPeers()` - DM users from config
- `listGroups()` - IRC channels from config

#### **g) Status** (connection health)
- `probeAccount()` - Test IRC connection
- `resolveAccountSnapshot()` - Current account state
- `buildChannelSummary()` - Display info

#### **h) Gateway** (lifecycle)
```typescript
gateway: {
  startAccount: async (ctx) => {
    // Start monitoring IRC connection
    // Set up inbound message handler
    await monitorIrcProvider({ ... })
  }
}
```

#### **i) Pairing** (DM approval)
```typescript
pairing: {
  text: {
    idLabel: "ircUser",
    message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
    normalizeAllowEntry: normalizeIrcAllowEntry,
    notify: async ({ id, message }) => sendMessageIrc(id, message)
  }
}
```

#### **j) Security**
```typescript
security: {
  resolveDmPolicy: resolveIrcDmPolicy,  // "pairing", "allowlist", "open"
  collectWarnings: collectIrcSecurityWarnings  // TLS, NickServ warnings
}
```

#### **k) Outbound**
```typescript
outbound: {
  base: {
    deliveryMode: "direct",
    chunker: chunkTextForOutbound,  // Split long messages
    chunkerMode: "markdown",
    textChunkLimit: 350
  },
  attachedResults: {
    channel: "irc",
    sendText: sendMessageIrc,  // Core calls this
    sendMedia: async ({ ..., mediaUrl }) => 
      sendMessageIrc(to, `${text}\n\nAttachment: ${mediaUrl}`)
  }
}
```

### 4. **Configuration Types** (`src/types.ts`)

```typescript
IrcAccountConfig {
  name?: string
  enabled?: boolean
  host?: string              // IRC server hostname
  port?: number              // IRC port (6667 plain, 6697 TLS)
  tls?: boolean
  nick?: string              // Bot nickname
  username?: string          // IDENT username
  realname?: string          // Real name field
  password?: string          // Server password
  passwordFile?: string      // Read from file
  nickserv?: IrcNickServConfig  // NickServ auth
  
  dmPolicy?: DmPolicy        // "pairing" | "allowlist" | "open"
  allowFrom?: string[]       // DM allowlist
  defaultTo?: string         // Default target
  
  groupPolicy?: GroupPolicy  // "allowlist" | "open" | "disabled"
  groupAllowFrom?: string[]  // Group-level allowlist
  groups?: Record<string, IrcChannelConfig>  // Per-channel config
  
  channels?: string[]        // Auto-join channels
  mentionPatterns?: string[] // Custom mention regexes
  markdown?: MarkdownConfig
  historyLimit?: number
  blockStreaming?: boolean
}

IrcConfig = IrcAccountConfig & {
  accounts?: Record<string, IrcAccountConfig>  // Multi-account
  defaultAccount?: string
}
```

### 5. **Inbound Message Handling** (`src/inbound.ts`)

When an IRC message arrives:

```typescript
async handleIrcInbound({
  message: IrcInboundMessage,
  account: ResolvedIrcAccount,
  config: CoreConfig,
  runtime: RuntimeEnv,
  connectedNick?: string,
  sendReply?: (...) => Promise<void>,
  statusSink?: (patch) => void
})
```

**Processing pipeline:**
1. Extract raw message text
2. Validate sender identity (nick, user@host)
3. Check DM allowlist policy (pairing/allowlist/open)
4. Check group/channel access (allowlist/open/disabled)
5. Apply mention gates (require @mentions in channels)
6. Dispatch to core via `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase()`
7. Handle tool results
8. Send reply back via `sendMessageIrc()`
9. Record activity timestamps

### 6. **Outbound Message Sending** (`src/send.ts`)

```typescript
async sendMessageIrc(
  to: string,
  text: string,
  opts?: {
    cfg?: CoreConfig
    accountId?: string
    replyTo?: string
    client?: IrcClient
  }
): Promise<SendIrcResult>
```

**Logic:**
1. Resolve account (use provided or from runtime config)
2. Validate IRC is configured
3. Normalize target (channel/nick)
4. Convert markdown tables
5. Append reply metadata if `replyTo`
6. Either send via existing client or create transient connection
7. Record outbound activity

### 7. **Account Resolution** (`src/accounts.ts`)

```typescript
resolveIrcAccount({ cfg, accountId }): ResolvedIrcAccount
```

**Returns:**
- `accountId`, `enabled`, `name`
- `configured`: true if host + nick present
- `host`, `port`, `tls`, `nick`, `username`, `realname`
- `password` (resolved from env/file/config)
- `passwordSource` (tracks where password came from)
- `config`: full config object

**Env var support:**
- `IRC_HOST`, `IRC_NICK`, `IRC_USERNAME`, `IRC_REALNAME`
- `IRC_PORT`, `IRC_TLS`, `IRC_PASSWORD`
- `IRC_CHANNELS`, `IRC_NICKSERV_PASSWORD`

### 8. **Connection Monitoring** (`src/monitor.ts`)

```typescript
async monitorIrcProvider(opts: IrcMonitorOptions)
```

**Lifecycle:**
1. Connect to IRC server
2. Join auto-configured channels
3. Listen for PRIVMSG events
4. Parse incoming messages
5. Call `handleIrcInbound()` for each message
6. Track status updates (last inbound/outbound time)
7. Clean up on abort signal

**Event types:**
- `onPrivmsg`: User message received
- `onNotice`: Server notice
- `onLine`: Raw protocol line (verbose logging)
- `onError`: Connection error

### 9. **Setup Flow** (`src/setup-core.ts` + `src/setup-surface.ts`)

**Core Setup Adapter** (`setup-core.ts`):
- `validateInput()`: Ensure host and nick are provided
- `applyAccountConfig()`: Apply form values to config
- Port parsing (default 6667 plain, 6697 TLS)
- NickServ configuration

**Interactive Wizard** (`setup-surface.ts`):
- Status: "configured" if host+nick present
- Intro note explaining TLS, NickServ, groups
- Text inputs: host, port, nick, username, realname
- Auto-join channels input
- Group access policy (allowlist/open/disabled)
- DM allowlist (pairing/allowlist/open)
- NickServ setup prompt
- Finalize: ask about mention requirements

### 10. **IRC Protocol Client** (`src/client.ts`)

```typescript
connectIrcClient(options: IrcClientOptions): Promise<IrcClient>
```

**Client interface:**
```typescript
type IrcClient = {
  nick: string
  isReady(): boolean
  sendRaw(line: string): void
  join(channel: string): void
  sendPrivmsg(target: string, text: string): void
  quit(reason?: string): void
  close(): void
}
```

**Features:**
- TLS/plaintext connections
- Connection timeout handling
- NickServ IDENTIFY/REGISTER
- Channel auto-join
- Nick collision fallback
- Raw line callbacks (for logging)

### 11. **Runtime Store** (`src/runtime.ts`)

```typescript
const { setRuntime: setIrcRuntime, getRuntime: getIrcRuntime } =
  createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("IRC runtime not initialized")
```

**Why:**
- Plugin needs access to core runtime (config, logging, activity tracking)
- Set during plugin initialization
- Used by inbound/outbound handlers

### 12. **API Barrels**

**`api.ts`** (Public exports for core):
- `listIrcAccountIds()`
- `resolveIrcAccount()`
- Setup wizard and adapter

**`runtime-api.ts`** (Private barrel):
- Re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`
- Helpers for status, activity, pairing, policy
- Types: `ChannelPlugin`, `PluginRuntime`, `BaseProbeResult`

**`channel-config-api.ts`** (Config schema):
- Exports `IrcChannelConfigSchema` for config UI

### 13. **Config Schema** (`src/config-schema.ts`)

Uses Zod for validation:
- `IrcGroupSchema`: per-channel config (mention, tools, allowFrom)
- `IrcNickServSchema`: NickServ settings
- `IrcAccountSchemaBase`: main account fields
- `IrcAccountSchema`: with validation (e.g., open dmPolicy requires "*" allowFrom)
- `IrcConfigSchema`: top-level config with accounts
- `IrcChannelConfigSchema`: final schema with UI hints

### 14. **Testing Patterns** (15 test files)

Examples show how to test:
- Outbound chunking without runtime
- Protocol parsing
- Normalization logic
- Account resolution with env vars
- Inbound message handling
- Setup validation
- Config schema

**Test structure:**
```typescript
import { describe, it, expect, afterEach } from "vitest"
import { ircPlugin } from "./channel.js"
import { clearIrcRuntime } from "./runtime.js"

describe("irc outbound chunking", () => {
  afterEach(() => clearIrcRuntime())
  
  it("chunks outbound text", () => {
    const chunker = ircPlugin.outbound?.chunker
    expect(chunker("alpha beta", 5)).toEqual(["alpha", "beta"])
  })
})
```

### 15. **Key Implementation Insights**

1. **No tsconfig.json per plugin** - Uses project root tsconfig
2. **Multi-account support** - Each IRC network is a separate account
3. **DM security model:**
   - Pairing (approval flow)
   - Allowlist (explicit nicks/hosts)
   - Open (accept all, with mention gates in channels)
4. **Markdown table conversion** - Configurable per account
5. **Reply metadata** - `[reply:messageId]` appended to outbound
6. **Message chunking** - 350 char default, respects markdown
7. **Activity tracking** - Records inbound/outbound timestamps
8. **Status probes** - Measure connection latency
9. **Security warnings** - TLS disabled, NickServ config issues
10. **Case-insensitive group matching** - Handles IRC's case rules

### 16. **Replication Checklist for PulseBoard Plugin**

To create your own channel plugin, follow this pattern:

```
1. Create /extensions/pulseBoard/
2. Create index.ts → defineChannelPluginEntry with plugin + setRuntime
3. Create package.json with openclaw.channel metadata
4. Create src/channel.ts → createChatChannelPlugin({ base, ... })
5. Create src/types.ts → your config types
6. Create src/accounts.ts → resolve account + list accounts
7. Create src/runtime.ts → createPluginRuntimeStore
8. Create src/send.ts → outbound message function
9. Create src/inbound.ts → handleInbound message processor
10. Create src/monitor.ts → connection lifecycle + message listener
11. Create src/client.ts → raw platform client (if needed)
12. Create src/setup-core.ts → apply config logic
13. Create src/setup-surface.ts → interactive wizard
14. Create src/config-schema.ts → Zod schema
15. Create src/normalize.ts → target/entry normalization
16. Create src/policy.ts → security/access control
17. Create api.ts → export public functions
18. Create runtime-api.ts → export SDK re-exports
19. Create setup-entry.ts → setupEntry
20. Create channel-config-api.ts → config schema export
```

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me create the detailed summary:

## Deep Dive: IRC Channel Plugin Implementation

I've thoroughly analyzed the IRC plugin and compared it with other channel plugins (Nostr, Discord). Here's the complete implementation pattern:

### 1. **File Structure Overview**

The IRC plugin has a clean, modular architecture:

```
/extensions/irc/
├── index.ts                      # Main entry point
├── api.ts                        # Public API barrel (accounts, setup-surface)
├── channel-config-api.ts         # Config schema export
├── runtime-api.ts                # Private runtime barrel (SDK re-exports)
├── setup-entry.ts                # Setup plugin entry
├── package.json                  # Package metadata + openclaw config
├── openclaw.plugin.json          # Plugin manifest
└── src/
    ├── channel.ts                # Core ChannelPlugin definition
    ├── runtime.ts                # Runtime store (getIrcRuntime, setIrcRuntime)
    ├── types.ts                  # TypeScript type definitions
    ├── accounts.ts               # Account resolution & listing
    ├── send.ts                   # Outbound message sending
    ├── inbound.ts                # Inbound message handling
    ├── monitor.ts                # Connection monitoring & lifecycle
    ├── client.ts                 # Raw IRC protocol client
    ├── protocol.ts               # IRC message parsing/serialization
    ├── normalize.ts              # Target/entry normalization
    ├── policy.ts                 # Security & access control
    ├── config-schema.ts          # Zod schema definitions
    ├── setup-core.ts             # Core setup logic
    ├── setup-surface.ts          # Interactive setup wizard
    ├── probe.ts                  # Connection testing
    ├── control-chars.ts          # IRC control character handling
    ├── connect-options.ts        # Connection configuration builder
    ├── config-ui-hints.ts        # UI hints for config
    └── *.test.ts                 # 15+ test files
```

### 2. **Registration Pattern**

**`index.ts` - Main Entry Point:**
The plugin registers via `defineChannelPluginEntry()`:
- Exports the `ircPlugin` from `channel.ts`
- Exports the `setIrcRuntime` from `runtime.ts`
- Metadata: `id: "irc"`, `name: "IRC"`, description
- Returns default export with `id`, `name`, `description`, `plugin`, `setRuntime`

**`package.json` - OpenClaw Metadata:**
```json
{
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "detailLabel": "IRC",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing...",
      "systemImage": "network"
    }
  }
}
```

**`setup-entry.ts`:**
Simply wraps the plugin for setup via `defineSetupPluginEntry(ircPlugin)`

### 3. **Core Channel Plugin Definition** (`src/channel.ts`)

The `ircPlugin` is created via `createChatChannelPlugin()` with these major sections:

#### **a) Base Configuration**
```typescript
base: {
  id: "irc",
  meta: { ...getChatChannelMeta("irc"), quickstartAllowFrom: true },
  setup: ircSetupAdapter,           // How to apply user input to config
  setupWizard: ircSetupWizard,      // Interactive setup prompts
  capabilities: {
    chatTypes: ["direct", "group"],
    media: true,
    blockStreaming: true
  },
  reload: { configPrefixes: ["channels.irc"] },
  configSchema: IrcChannelConfigSchema // Zod schema
}
```

#### **b) Config Adapter** (resolves account, validates setup)
- `listAccountIds()` - List all IRC accounts
- `resolveAccount()` - Get account config with defaults
- `isConfigured()` - Check if account has required fields (host, nick)
- `describeAccount()` - Human-readable account summary
- `defaultAccountId` - Fallback account

#### **c) Groups** (channel access control)
```typescript
groups: {
  resolveRequireMention({ cfg, accountId, groupId })
  resolveToolPolicy({ cfg, accountId, groupId })
}
```
Controls per-channel mention requirements and tool policies

#### **d) Messaging** (target normalization)
```typescript
messaging: {
  normalizeTarget: normalizeIrcMessagingTarget,  // "#channel" or "nick"
  targetResolver: {
    looksLikeId: looksLikeIrcTargetId,
    hint: "<#channel|nick>"
  }
}
```

#### **e) Resolver** (convert user input to message targets)
- `resolveTargets()` - Map inputs to IRC targets (channels get `#`, nicks as-is)
- Validates kind (group vs user)

#### **f) Directory** (peer/group listing)
- `listPeers()` - DM users from config
- `listGroups()` - IRC channels from config

#### **g) Status** (connection health)
- `probeAccount()` - Test IRC connection
- `resolveAccountSnapshot()` - Current account state
- `buildChannelSummary()` - Display info

#### **h) Gateway** (lifecycle)
```typescript
gateway: {
  startAccount: async (ctx) => {
    // Start monitoring IRC connection
    // Set up inbound message handler
    await monitorIrcProvider({ ... })
  }
}
```

#### **i) Pairing** (DM approval)
```typescript
pairing: {
  text: {
    idLabel: "ircUser",
    message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
    normalizeAllowEntry: normalizeIrcAllowEntry,
    notify: async ({ id, message }) => sendMessageIrc(id, message)
  }
}
```

#### **j) Security**
```typescript
security: {
  resolveDmPolicy: resolveIrcDmPolicy,  // "pairing", "allowlist", "open"
  collectWarnings: collectIrcSecurityWarnings  // TLS, NickServ warnings
}
```

#### **k) Outbound**
```typescript
outbound: {
  base: {
    deliveryMode: "direct",
    chunker: chunkTextForOutbound,  // Split long messages
    chunkerMode: "markdown",
    textChunkLimit: 350
  },
  attachedResults: {
    channel: "irc",
    sendText: sendMessageIrc,  // Core calls this
    sendMedia: async ({ ..., mediaUrl }) => 
      sendMessageIrc(to, `${text}\n\nAttachment: ${mediaUrl}`)
  }
}
```

content.content:
1	import {
2	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
3	  BlockStreamingCoalesceSchema,
4	  DmConfigSchema,
5	  DmPolicySchema,
6	  GroupPolicySchema,
7	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
8	  ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
9	  ToolPolicySchema,
10	  requireOpenAllowFrom,
11	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema";
12	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
13	import { ircChannelConfigUiHints } from "./config-ui-hints.js";
14	
15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
80	  });
81	});
82	
83	export const IrcConfigSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.extend({
84	  accounts: z.record(z.string(), IrcAccountSchema.optional()).optional(),
85	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
86	}).superRefine((value, ctx) => {
87	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
88	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
89	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
90	    ctx,
91	    path: ["allowFrom"],
92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
93	  });
94	});
95	
96	export const IrcChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(IrcConfigSchema, {
97	  uiHints: ircChannelConfigUiHints,
98	});

// Private runtime barrel for the bundled IRC extension.
// Keep this barrel thin and aligned with the local extension surface.

export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  createChannelPairingController,
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments,
  dispatchInboundReplyWithBase,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL,
  isDangerousNameMatchingEnabled,
  logInboundDrop,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,
  resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy,
  resolveControlCommandGate,
  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists,
  warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  BlockStreamingCoalesceConfig,
  ChannelPlugin,
  DmConfig,
  DmPolicy,
  GroupPolicy,
  GroupToolPolicyBySenderConfig,
  GroupToolPolicyConfig,
  MarkdownConfig,
  OpenClawConfig,
  OutboundReplyPayload,
  PluginRuntime,
  RuntimeEnv,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";

content.text: Now I have a thorough understanding of the complete channel plugin structure. Let me launch a Plan agent to design the implementation:

input.prompt:
I need to design a detailed implementation plan for a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel plugin for the OpenClaw platform. PulseBoard is an imaginary real-time bulletin board messaging platform where users post messages to named "boards" and can send direct messages.

**Requirements:**
- Support both "direct" and "group" chat types
- 4000-character text chunk limit
- Follow the same extension structure as existing channel plugins (IRC, Nostr)
- Only create NEW files in `extensions/pulseboard/` — do NOT modify any existing files

**Existing Plugin Structure (from IRC):**

The plugin directory structure follows this pattern:
```
extensions/<id>/
├── index.ts                     # defineChannelPluginEntry() — main entry
├── setup-entry.ts               # defineSetupPluginEntry() — setup wizard entry
├── api.ts                       # Public barrel (accounts, setup-surface)
├── runtime-api.ts               # Private runtime barrel (SDK re-exports)  [NOTE: for IRC this is at src/runtime-api.ts]
├── channel-config-api.ts        # Config schema export barrel
├── openclaw.plugin.json         # Manifest
├── package.json                 # NPM metadata + openclaw block
└── src/
    ├── channel.ts               # createChatChannelPlugin() — the main plugin definition
    ├── runtime.ts               # createPluginRuntimeStore() — runtime singleton
    ├── types.ts                 # TypeScript interfaces for config, accounts, messages
    ├── accounts.ts              # Account resolution + listing helpers
    ├── send.ts                  # Outbound message sending
    ├── inbound.ts               # Inbound message handler
    ├── monitor.ts               # Connection monitoring & lifecycle
    ├── normalize.ts             # Target/entry normalization
    ├── policy.ts                # Group access policy resolution
    ├── config-schema.ts         # Zod config schemas
    ├── config-ui-hints.ts       # UI hints for config fields
    ├── setup-core.ts            # ChannelSetupAdapter (apply config logic)
    ├── setup-surface.ts         # ChannelSetupWizard (interactive wizard)
    └── *.test.ts                # Tests colocated with source
```

**Key patterns from IRC plugin:**

1. **`index.ts`** exports default `defineChannelPluginEntry({ id, name, description, plugin, setRuntime })` with the plugin cast as `ChannelPlugin`.

2. **`setup-entry.ts`** exports default `defineSetupPluginEntry(plugin)`.

3. **`package.json`** has `openclaw.extensions`, `openclaw.setupEntry`, `openclaw.channel` (id, label, selectionLabel, blurb, docsPath, etc.), and `openclaw.install.minHostVersion`.

4. **`openclaw.plugin.json`** has `id`, `channels: [id]`, `configSchema` (JSON Schema object).

5. **`src/channel.ts`** uses `createChatChannelPlugin()` with:
   - `base.id`, `base.meta` (from `getChatChannelMeta`), `base.capabilities` (`chatTypes: ["direct","group"]`, `media: true`, `blockStreaming: true`), `base.reload`, `base.configSchema`, `base.config`, `base.groups`, `base.messaging`, `base.resolver`, `base.directory`, `base.status`, `base.gateway`, `base.setup`, `base.setupWizard`
   - `pairing` section (text-based pairing)
   - `security` section (DM policy + warnings)
   - `outbound` section (deliveryMode, chunker, textChunkLimit, sendText, sendMedia)

6. **`src/runtime.ts`** uses `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("... runtime not initialized")`.

7. **`src/types.ts`** defines config types (`PulseBoardAccountConfig`, `PulseBoardConfig`, `CoreConfig`), inbound message type, and probe type.

8. **`src/accounts.ts`** uses `createAccountListHelpers`, `resolveMergedAccountConfig`, env var resolution, password/token resolution.

9. **`src/send.ts`** resolves account, normalizes target, sends message via client, records outbound activity.

10. **`src/normalize.ts`** normalizes messaging targets (boards use `#` prefix like IRC channels), validates target format.

11. **`src/policy.ts`** resolves group matching, group access gates, mention gates.

12. **`src/config-schema.ts`** uses Zod schemas with `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `GroupPolicySchema`, etc.

13. **`src/setup-core.ts`** implements `ChannelSetupAdapter` with `resolveAccountId`, `validateInput`, `applyAccountConfig`.

14. **`src/setup-surface.ts`** implements `ChannelSetupWizard` with status, introNote, prepare, credentials, textInputs, groupAccess, allowFrom, finalize, dmPolicy.

15. **`src/runtime-api.ts`** (at `src/` level) re-exports SDK helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<id>`. Since PulseBoard is new and won't have its own SDK subpath, this should re-export from generic channel helpers like `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` etc. — OR we can use the IRC pattern where it re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` since that exposes generic helpers. Actually, for a new plugin without a dedicated SDK subpath, we should import directly from the generic `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths.

**PulseBoard-specific design:**

- **Platform concept**: Boards are group conversations (like `#board-name`), DMs are direct conversations to user IDs
- **Auth**: API token + board URL (fictional)
- **Config keys**: `channels.pulseboard.*`
- **Env vars**: `PULSEBOARD_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`, `PULSEBOARD_BOARDS`
- **Group target prefix**: `#` (like `#general`, `#announcements`)
- **Text chunk limit**: 4000 characters
- **Config**: URL, token, boards to join, dmPolicy, allowFrom, groupPolicy, groups, etc.

Please provide a detailed step-by-step implementation plan listing every file to create, what each file should contain (with key function signatures, types, and SDK imports), and any specific patterns to follow. Keep it concise but complete enough that someone could implement it without re-reading the codebase.

Note: since PulseBoard is fictional, the `client.ts` and `monitor.ts` can have stub/placeholder implementations that log but don't actually connect to anything real.

Important: we are ONLY creating new files. No modifications to existing files anywhere in the repo.

28	  .object({

31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),

35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({

41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }

48	  .object({

tool_use_result.prompt:
I need to design a detailed implementation plan for a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel plugin for the OpenClaw platform. PulseBoard is an imaginary real-time bulletin board messaging platform where users post messages to named "boards" and can send direct messages.

**Requirements:**
- Support both "direct" and "group" chat types
- 4000-character text chunk limit
- Follow the same extension structure as existing channel plugins (IRC, Nostr)
- Only create NEW files in `extensions/pulseboard/` — do NOT modify any existing files

**Existing Plugin Structure (from IRC):**

The plugin directory structure follows this pattern:
```
extensions/<id>/
├── index.ts                     # defineChannelPluginEntry() — main entry
├── setup-entry.ts               # defineSetupPluginEntry() — setup wizard entry
├── api.ts                       # Public barrel (accounts, setup-surface)
├── runtime-api.ts               # Private runtime barrel (SDK re-exports)  [NOTE: for IRC this is at src/runtime-api.ts]
├── channel-config-api.ts        # Config schema export barrel
├── openclaw.plugin.json         # Manifest
├── package.json                 # NPM metadata + openclaw block
└── src/
    ├── channel.ts               # createChatChannelPlugin() — the main plugin definition
    ├── runtime.ts               # createPluginRuntimeStore() — runtime singleton
    ├── types.ts                 # TypeScript interfaces for config, accounts, messages
    ├── accounts.ts              # Account resolution + listing helpers
    ├── send.ts                  # Outbound message sending
    ├── inbound.ts               # Inbound message handler
    ├── monitor.ts               # Connection monitoring & lifecycle
    ├── normalize.ts             # Target/entry normalization
    ├── policy.ts                # Group access policy resolution
    ├── config-schema.ts         # Zod config schemas
    ├── config-ui-hints.ts       # UI hints for config fields
    ├── setup-core.ts            # ChannelSetupAdapter (apply config logic)
    ├── setup-surface.ts         # ChannelSetupWizard (interactive wizard)
    └── *.test.ts                # Tests colocated with source
```

**Key patterns from IRC plugin:**

1. **`index.ts`** exports default `defineChannelPluginEntry({ id, name, description, plugin, setRuntime })` with the plugin cast as `ChannelPlugin`.

2. **`setup-entry.ts`** exports default `defineSetupPluginEntry(plugin)`.

3. **`package.json`** has `openclaw.extensions`, `openclaw.setupEntry`, `openclaw.channel` (id, label, selectionLabel, blurb, docsPath, etc.), and `openclaw.install.minHostVersion`.

4. **`openclaw.plugin.json`** has `id`, `channels: [id]`, `configSchema` (JSON Schema object).

5. **`src/channel.ts`** uses `createChatChannelPlugin()` with:
   - `base.id`, `base.meta` (from `getChatChannelMeta`), `base.capabilities` (`chatTypes: ["direct","group"]`, `media: true`, `blockStreaming: true`), `base.reload`, `base.configSchema`, `base.config`, `base.groups`, `base.messaging`, `base.resolver`, `base.directory`, `base.status`, `base.gateway`, `base.setup`, `base.setupWizard`
   - `pairing` section (text-based pairing)
   - `security` section (DM policy + warnings)
   - `outbound` section (deliveryMode, chunker, textChunkLimit, sendText, sendMedia)

6. **`src/runtime.ts`** uses `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("... runtime not initialized")`.

7. **`src/types.ts`** defines config types (`PulseBoardAccountConfig`, `PulseBoardConfig`, `CoreConfig`), inbound message type, and probe type.

8. **`src/accounts.ts`** uses `createAccountListHelpers`, `resolveMergedAccountConfig`, env var resolution, password/token resolution.

9. **`src/send.ts`** resolves account, normalizes target, sends message via client, records outbound activity.

10. **`src/normalize.ts`** normalizes messaging targets (boards use `#` prefix like IRC channels), validates target format.

11. **`src/policy.ts`** resolves group matching, group access gates, mention gates.

12. **`src/config-schema.ts`** uses Zod schemas with `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `GroupPolicySchema`, etc.

13. **`src/setup-core.ts`** implements `ChannelSetupAdapter` with `resolveAccountId`, `validateInput`, `applyAccountConfig`.

14. **`src/setup-surface.ts`** implements `ChannelSetupWizard` with status, introNote, prepare, credentials, textInputs, groupAccess, allowFrom, finalize, dmPolicy.

15. **`src/runtime-api.ts`** (at `src/` level) re-exports SDK helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<id>`. Since PulseBoard is new and won't have its own SDK subpath, this should re-export from generic channel helpers like `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` etc. — OR we can use the IRC pattern where it re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` since that exposes generic helpers. Actually, for a new plugin without a dedicated SDK subpath, we should import directly from the generic `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths.

**PulseBoard-specific design:**

- **Platform concept**: Boards are group conversations (like `#board-name`), DMs are direct conversations to user IDs
- **Auth**: API token + board URL (fictional)
- **Config keys**: `channels.pulseboard.*`
- **Env vars**: `PULSEBOARD_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`, `PULSEBOARD_BOARDS`
- **Group target prefix**: `#` (like `#general`, `#announcements`)
- **Text chunk limit**: 4000 characters
- **Config**: URL, token, boards to join, dmPolicy, allowFrom, groupPolicy, groups, etc.

Please provide a detailed step-by-step implementation plan listing every file to create, what each file should contain (with key function signatures, types, and SDK imports), and any specific patterns to follow. Keep it concise but complete enough that someone could implement it without re-reading the codebase.

Note: since PulseBoard is fictional, the `client.ts` and `monitor.ts` can have stub/placeholder implementations that log but don't actually connect to anything real.

Important: we are ONLY creating new files. No modifications to existing files anywhere in the repo.

content.text: Good — `extensions/*` is in the pnpm workspace, so any new `extensions/pulseboard/` directory with a `package.json` will be discovered automatically. I also confirmed the available SDK subpaths. Now let me check one more thing — how a newer/simpler plugin like Mattermost handles the meta construction inline (since `getChatChannelMeta` won't know about "pulseboard"):

# Plan: Add PulseBoard Channel Plugin

## Context

PulseBoard is a fictional real-time bulletin board messaging platform. We need to add it as a new bundled channel plugin at `extensions/pulseboard/`. The plugin supports both direct and group chat types with a 4000-character text chunk limit. **Only new files are created — no existing files are modified.**

The implementation closely follows the IRC plugin pattern (`extensions/irc/`) as the primary reference, with the Mattermost plugin's inline meta construction (`extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts:210-221`) as reference for the meta object (since `getChatChannelMeta` doesn't know about "pulseboard").

---

## Files to Create (21 files)

All paths are relative to repo root, under `extensions/pulseboard/`.

### Step 1: Scaffolding — `package.json` + `openclaw.plugin.json`

**`extensions/pulseboard/package.json`**
- `name`: `"@openclaw/pulseboard"`
- `version`: `"2026.4.1-beta.1"` (match other extensions)
- `description`: `"OpenClaw PulseBoard channel plugin"`
- `type`: `"module"`
- `devDependencies`: `{ "openclaw": "workspace:*" }`
- `peerDependencies`: `{ "openclaw": ">=2026.4.1-beta.1" }`
- `openclaw.extensions`: `["./index.ts"]`
- `openclaw.setupEntry`: `"./setup-entry.ts"`
- `openclaw.channel`: `{ id: "pulseboard", label: "PulseBoard", selectionLabel: "PulseBoard (URL + Token)", detailLabel: "PulseBoard", docsPath: "/channels/pulseboard", docsLabel: "pulseboard", blurb: "real-time bulletin board messaging with boards and DMs.", systemImage: "list.bullet.rectangle" }`

**`extensions/pulseboard/openclaw.plugin.json`**
- `id`: `"pulseboard"`
- `channels`: `["pulseboard"]`
- `configSchema`: `{ "type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "properties": {} }`

### Step 2: Type Definitions — `src/types.ts`

Reference: `extensions/irc/src/types.ts`

Define:
- `PulseBoardGroupConfig` — `{ requireMention?, tools?, enabled?, allowFrom?, systemPrompt? }`
- `PulseBoardAccountConfig` — `{ name?, enabled?, url?, token?, tokenFile?, boards?, dmPolicy?, allowFrom?, defaultTo?, groupPolicy?, groupAllowFrom?, groups?, mentionPatterns?, textChunkLimit? }`
- `PulseBoardConfig` — extends `PulseBoardAccountConfig` with `{ accounts?, defaultAccount? }`
- `CoreConfig` — `OpenClawConfig & { channels?: { pulseboard?: PulseBoardConfig } }`
- `PulseBoardInboundMessage` — `{ messageId, target, senderId, senderName?, text, timestamp, isGroup }`
- `PulseBoardProbe` — `BaseProbeResult<string> & { url, latencyMs? }`

Import `OpenClawConfig` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime`, other shared types (`DmPolicy`, `GroupPolicy`, `GroupToolPolicyConfig`, `BaseProbeResult`) from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema` / `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers`.

### Step 3: Runtime Barrel + Store — `src/runtime-api.ts` + `src/runtime.ts`

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime-api.ts`**
Re-export generic SDK helpers that the plugin needs internally. Since there's no `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard` subpath, import from individual SDK entrypoints:
- `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-status`
- `buildBaseChannelStatusSummary` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers`
- `createAccountStatusSink` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-runtime`
- `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id`
- Type re-exports: `ChannelPlugin` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, `PluginRuntime` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime`, `RuntimeEnv` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-env`

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime.ts`**
Reference: `extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts`
- Use `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized")`
- Export `getPulseBoardRuntime`, `setPulseBoardRuntime`, `clearPulseBoardRuntime`

### Step 4: Utilities — `src/text-utils.ts` + `src/normalize.ts`

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/text-utils.ts`**
Local `chunkTextForOutbound(text: string, limit: number): string[]` implementation (not available as a public entrypoint). Split at newlines first, then spaces, falling back to hard break at limit.

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/normalize.ts`**
Reference: `extensions/irc/src/normalize.ts`
- `isBoardTarget(target: string): boolean` — returns `target.startsWith("#")`
- `normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget(raw: string): string | undefined` — strip `pulseboard:` / `board:` / `user:` prefixes, validate with `/^[^\s:]+$/u`
- `looksLikePulseBoardTargetId(raw: string): boolean` — pattern check
- `normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry(raw: string): string` — lowercase, strip prefixes
- `normalizePulseBoardAllowlist(entries?: Array<string | number>): string[]`

### Step 5: Policy — `src/policy.ts`

Reference: `extensions/irc/src/policy.ts`

- `PulseBoardGroupMatch` type + `resolvePulseBoardGroupMatch({ groups, target })` — case-insensitive key match with `*` wildcard fallback
- `PulseBoardGroupAccessGate` type + `resolvePulseBoardGroupAccessGate({ groupPolicy, groupMatch })` — disabled/allowlist/open logic
- `resolvePulseBoardRequireMention({ groupConfig?, wildcardConfig? }): boolean` — default `true`
- `resolvePulseBoardMentionGate(...)` — skip if no mention in group when required
- `resolvePulseBoardGroupSenderAllowed(...)` — allowlist matching using simple user ID equality (no host segments)

### Step 6: Account Resolution — `src/accounts.ts`

Reference: `extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts`

- `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount` type: `{ accountId, enabled, name?, configured, url, token, tokenSource, config }`
- `listPulseBoardAccountIds` + `resolveDefaultPulseBoardAccountId` via `createAccountListHelpers("pulseboard", { normalizeAccountId })`
  - Import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers` and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id`
- `resolvePulseBoardAccount({ cfg, accountId? }): ResolvedPulseBoardAccount`
  - Uses `resolveMergedAccountConfig` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution`
  - Env vars: `PULSEBOARD_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`, `PULSEBOARD_BOARDS`
  - Token resolution: env → tokenFile → config (using `tryReadSecretFileSync` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input`)
  - `configured = Boolean(url && token)`
- `listEnabledPulseBoardAccounts(cfg): ResolvedPulseBoardAccount[]`

### Step 7: Stub Client — `src/client.ts`

Fictional stub — logs actions but doesn't connect to anything real.

- `PulseBoardMessageEvent` type: `{ senderId, senderName?, target, text }`
- `PulseBoardClientOptions` type: `{ url, token, boards?, abortSignal?, onMessage?, onError? }`
- `PulseBoardClient` type: `{ isReady(), sendMessage(target, text), joinBoard(board), disconnect(reason?) }`
- `connectPulseBoardClient(opts): Promise<PulseBoardClient>` — returns stub client that logs via console

### Step 8: Send — `src/send.ts`

Reference: `extensions/irc/src/send.ts`

- `SendPulseBoardResult` type: `{ messageId, target }`
- `sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, opts?): Promise<SendPulseBoardResult>`
  - Resolve account, normalize target, convert markdown tables (from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime` + `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-runtime`)
  - Connect stub client, send
  - Record outbound activity via runtime
  - Return `{ messageId: crypto.randomUUID(), target }`

### Step 9: Inbound — `src/inbound.ts`

Reference: `extensions/irc/src/inbound.ts`

- `handlePulseBoardInbound({ message, account, config, runtime, sendReply?, statusSink? }): Promise<void>`
  - Resolve DM/group policy gates using functions from `policy.ts`
  - For DMs: check allowlist, initiate pairing if needed
  - For groups: check group access gate, sender allowlist, mention gate
  - Dispatch inbound to core via runtime channel reply APIs
  - Deliver reply via `sendReply` callback or `sendMessagePulseBoard`

### Step 10: Monitor — `src/monitor.ts`

Reference: `extensions/irc/src/monitor.ts`

- `PulseBoardMonitorOptions` type: `{ accountId?, config?, runtime?, abortSignal?, statusSink? }`
- `monitorPulseBoardProvider(opts): Promise<{ stop: () => void }>`
  - Resolve account, check configured
  - Connect stub client, join boards from `account.config.boards`
  - Wire `onMessage` → `handlePulseBoardInbound`
  - Return stop function

### Step 11: Config Schema + UI Hints — `src/config-schema.ts` + `src/config-ui-hints.ts`

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/config-ui-hints.ts`**
Reference: `extensions/irc/src/config-ui-hints.ts`
- `pulseBoardChannelConfigUiHints` — record with `""` (root label), `dmPolicy` hint

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/config-schema.ts`**
Reference: `extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts`
- Imports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema`: `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `GroupPolicySchema`, `ToolPolicySchema`, `requireOpenAllowFrom`
- `z` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod`
- `PulseBoardGroupSchema` — strict Zod object
- `PulseBoardAccountSchemaBase` — strict Zod object with all account fields
- `PulseBoardAccountSchema` — with `requireOpenAllowFrom` superRefine
- `PulseBoardConfigSchema` — extends base with accounts + defaultAccount
- `PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema` — `buildChannelConfigSchema(PulseBoardConfigSchema, { uiHints })`

### Step 12: Setup — `src/setup-core.ts` + `src/setup-surface.ts`

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-core.ts`**
Reference: `extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts`
- `updatePulseBoardAccountConfig(cfg, accountId, patch): CoreConfig` via `patchScopedAccountConfig`
- `setPulseBoardDmPolicy` / `setPulseBoardAllowFrom` via `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter` / `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`
- `setPulseBoardGroupAccess(cfg, accountId, policy, entries, normalizeGroupEntry): CoreConfig`
- `pulseBoardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter` — validates URL required, applies url/token/boards

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-surface.ts`**
Reference: `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts`
- `pulseBoardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard` with:
  - `status`: unconfiguredLabel "needs URL + token"
  - `introNote`: PulseBoard setup info, env vars
  - `prepare`: detect `PULSEBOARD_URL` + `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN` in env
  - `textInputs`: URL, token (sensitive), boards to join
  - `groupAccess`: board allowlist management
  - `allowFrom`: DM allowlist with simple user IDs
  - `dmPolicy`: `{ label: "PulseBoard", channel: "pulseboard", ... }`
  - `disable`: `setSetupChannelEnabled(cfg, "pulseboard", false)`

### Step 13: Main Plugin — `src/channel.ts`

Reference: `extensions/irc/src/channel.ts` (full structure), `extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts:210-221` (inline meta)

Build `pulseBoardPlugin` via `createChatChannelPlugin({...})`:

- **meta**: Inline object (not `getChatChannelMeta`): `{ id: "pulseboard", label: "PulseBoard", selectionLabel: "PulseBoard (URL + Token)", ... quickstartAllowFrom: true }`
- **capabilities**: `{ chatTypes: ["direct", "group"], media: false, blockStreaming: true }`
- **config adapter**: via `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter` with `sectionKey: "pulseboard"`
- **DM security**: via `createScopedDmSecurityResolver` with `channelKey: "pulseboard"`
- **security warnings**: via `composeAccountWarningCollectors` + `createAllowlistProviderOpenWarningCollector`
- **directory**: via `createChannelDirectoryAdapter` + `createResolvedDirectoryEntriesLister`
- **status**: via `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter` with probe and snapshot
- **gateway.startAccount**: check configured, call `runStoppablePassiveMonitor` → `monitorPulseBoardProvider`
- **pairing**: text-based with `idLabel: "pulseBoardUser"`, `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`
- **outbound**: `deliveryMode: "direct"`, `textChunkLimit: 4000`, local `chunkTextForOutbound`, `sendText`/`sendMedia` via `sendMessagePulseBoard`
- **groups**: `resolveRequireMention` + `resolveToolPolicy` using policy helpers
- **messaging**: `normalizeTarget: normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget`, hint `"<#board|userId>"`
- **resolver**: map inputs to board or user targets

### Step 14: Entry Points + Barrels

**`extensions/pulseboard/index.ts`**
Reference: `extensions/irc/index.ts`
```ts
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";
export { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setPulseBoardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";
export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "pulseboard",
  name: "PulseBoard",
  description: "PulseBoard channel plugin",
  plugin: pulseBoardPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setPulseBoardRuntime,
});
```

**`extensions/pulseboard/setup-entry.ts`**
```ts
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseBoardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin);
```

**`extensions/pulseboard/api.ts`**
```ts
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

**`extensions/pulseboard/channel-config-api.ts`**
```ts
export { PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema } from "./src/config-schema.js";
```

---

## SDK Imports Summary

All imports use **public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths** (verified against `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`):

| Import | Entrypoint |
|--------|-----------|
| `defineChannelPluginEntry`, `createChatChannelPlugin`, `ChannelConfigUiHint` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` |
| `defineSetupPluginEntry` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` |
| `createPluginRuntimeStore` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store` |
| `createAccountListHelpers` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers` |
| `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id` |
| `resolveMergedAccountConfig` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution` |
| `describeAccountSnapshot` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers` |
| `tryReadSecretFileSync`, `parseOptionalDelimitedEntries` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` |
| `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input` |
| `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`, `createScopedDmSecurityResolver`, `adaptScopedAccountAccessor` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` |
| `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `GroupPolicySchema`, `ToolPolicySchema`, `requireOpenAllowFrom` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema` |
| `z` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod` |
| `composeAccountWarningCollectors`, `createAllowlistProviderOpenWarningCollector` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-policy` |
| `formatNormalizedAllowFromEntries` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/allow-from` |
| `createChannelDirectoryAdapter`, `createResolvedDirectoryEntriesLister` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/directory-runtime` |
| `runStoppablePassiveMonitor`, `resolveLoggerBackedRuntime` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/extension-shared` |
| `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`, `createDefaultChannelRuntimeState`, `buildBaseChannelStatusSummary` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` |
| `createAccountStatusSink` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-runtime` |
| `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-status` |
| `resolveMarkdownTableMode` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime` |
| `convertMarkdownTables` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-runtime` |
| `ChannelSetupAdapter`, `ChannelSetupInput` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-setup` |
| setup helpers (`createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, `patchScopedAccountConfig`, etc.) | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup` |
| `PluginRuntime` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime` |
| `RuntimeEnv` | `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-env` |

**Local implementation needed**: `chunkTextForOutbound` — not exposed as a public entrypoint; implemented locally in `src/text-utils.ts`.

---

## Verification

1. **Type-check**: `pnpm tsgo` — ensures all SDK imports resolve and types are correct
2. **Lint/format**: `pnpm check` — validates formatting and lint rules
3. **Build**: `pnpm build` — verifies no `[INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT]` warnings
4. **Plugin discovery**: verify `extensions/pulseboard/` appears in workspace with `pnpm ls --filter @openclaw/pulseboard`
5. **Scoped test**: if we add test files, run `pnpm test -- extensions/pulseboard`

input.plan:
# Plan: Add PulseBoard Channel Plugin

## Context

PulseBoard is a fictional real-time bulletin board messaging platform. We need to add it as a new bundled channel plugin at `extensions/pulseboard/`. The plugin supports both direct and group chat types with a 4000-character text chunk limit. **Only new files are created — no existing files are modified.**

The implementation closely follows the IRC plugin pattern (`extensions/irc/`) as the primary reference, with the Mattermost plugin's inline meta construction (`extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts:210-221`) as reference for the meta object (since `getChatChannelMeta` doesn't know about "pulseboard").

---

## Files to Create (21 files)

All paths are relative to repo root, under `extensions/pulseboard/`.

### Step 1: Scaffolding — `package.json` + `openclaw.plugin.json`

**`extensions/pulseboard/package.json`**
- `name`: `"@openclaw/pulseboard"`
- `version`: `"2026.4.1-beta.1"` (match other extensions)
- `description`: `"OpenClaw PulseBoard channel plugin"`
- `type`: `"module"`
- `devDependencies`: `{ "openclaw": "workspace:*" }`
- `peerDependencies`: `{ "openclaw": ">=2026.4.1-beta.1" }`
- `openclaw.extensions`: `["./index.ts"]`
- `openclaw.setupEntry`: `"./setup-entry.ts"`
- `openclaw.channel`: `{ id: "pulseboard", label: "PulseBoard", selectionLabel: "PulseBoard (URL + Token)", detailLabel: "PulseBoard", docsPath: "/channels/pulseboard", docsLabel: "pulseboard", blurb: "real-time bulletin board messaging with boards and DMs.", systemImage: "list.bullet.rectangle" }`

**`extensions/pulseboard/openclaw.plugin.json`**
- `id`: `"pulseboard"`
- `channels`: `["pulseboard"]`
- `configSchema`: `{ "type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "properties": {} }`

### Step 2: Type Definitions — `src/types.ts`

Reference: `extensions/irc/src/types.ts`

Define:

- `PulseBoardAccountConfig` — `{ name?, enabled?, url?, token?, tokenFile?, boards?, dmPolicy?, allowFrom?, defaultTo?, groupPolicy?, groupAllowFrom?, groups?, mentionPatterns?, textChunkLimit? }`
- `PulseBoardConfig` — extends `PulseBoardAccountConfig` with `{ accounts?, defaultAccount? }`
- `CoreConfig` — `OpenClawConfig & { channels?: { pulseboard?: PulseBoardConfig } }`
- `PulseBoardInboundMessage` — `{ messageId, target, senderId, senderName?, text, timestamp, isGroup }`
- `PulseBoardProbe` — `BaseProbeResult<string> & { url, latencyMs? }`

Import `OpenClawConfig` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime`, other shared types (`DmPolicy`, `GroupPolicy`, `GroupToolPolicyConfig`, `BaseProbeResult`) from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema` / `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers`.

### Step 4: Utilities — `src/text-utils.ts` + `src/normalize.ts`

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/text-utils.ts`**
Local `chunkTextForOutbound(text: string, limit: number): string[]` implementation (not available as a public entrypoint). Split at newlines first, then spaces, falling back to hard break at limit.

**`extensions/pulseboard/src/normalize.ts`**
Reference: `extensions/irc/src/normalize.ts`
- `isBoardTarget(target: string): boolean` — returns `target.startsWith("#")`

  - Env vars: `PULSEBOARD_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`, `PULSEBOARD_BOARDS`
  - Token resolution: env → tokenFile → config (using `tryReadSecretFileSync` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input`)
  - `configured = Boolean(url && token)`
- `listEnabledPulseBoardAccounts(cfg): ResolvedPulseBoardAccount[]`

  - Resolve account, normalize target, convert markdown tables (from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime` + `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-runtime`)
